titleStmt.title: Letter from Matthew Nathan to Andrew Philip Magill, 19 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Matthew Nathan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Sir Matthew Nathan (1862–1939), the under-secretary for Ireland, to Andrew Phillip Magill (1871–1941), the private secretary to Augustine Birrell (1850–1933). Nathan continues to discuss the issue of two boys (one named Mulligan) who were fined for throwing stones.Nathan was under-secretary until the 1916 Rising, after which he offered his resignation.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/7801/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Chief Secretary's Office, Dublin Castle. April 19 th1916 My dear Magill, I agree as regards I & II.
Mulligan & the other boy were
not discharged from an industrial
school but were fined for
stone throwing. Field got an
answer with regard to them
in the H. of C. on the 12 th. He
has possibly forgotten all about
it. I annex papers. Yours sincerely Matthew Nathan
